title: Rest API methods failing
body:
### Issue Description

I am attempting to command podman through rest api/socket and I am facing some difficulties with it. I can retrieve a lot of information, no problems with that- but if I try to **do** something, meaning, stopping or starting containers, I get to nowhere. I have followed REST API documentation, but it's possible that syntax has changed and that's why I am failing- who knows, better to ask..

So..
```
root@localhost:/usr/src/podman-ubus2# podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND               CREATED       STATUS            PORTS       NAMES
3b0b1a36d6b4  docker.io/library/alpine:latest          sh                    2 days ago    Up 2 days                     agitated_ride
2986e8124885  localhost/podman-pause:4.4.2-1677575119                        10 hours ago  Up 10 hours                   2855d7df4082-infra
c6ee01f0f4ed  docker.io/oskarirauta/nginx:latest       nginx -g daemon o...  10 hours ago  Up About an hour              nginx
root@localhost:/usr/src/podman-ubus2# curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock d/v4.0.0/libpod/containers/nginx/stop        
Method Not Allowed
root@localhost:/usr/src/podman-ubus2# curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock d/v4.0.0/libpod/containers/c6ee01f0f4ed/stop
Method Not Allowed
root@localhost:/usr/src/podman-ubus2# 

```

It doesn't matter, whether I use name or id as selector, always result is Method Not Allowed. Containers can be inside pods, or independent; result is the same.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run a container- any container.
2.Send command to socket that stops/kills/starts/etc it


### Describe the results you received

Method Not Allowed

### Describe the results you expected

OK?

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

netavark instead of cni, internal firewall management disabled.

### Additional information

Issue occurs every time.

comments.body: It **might** be that my curl request must be done as post - not as get, as it propably does it.. Or is there something more to it than user error?
Closing this - my bad. If someone else finds one day to be in same situation, notice that call should use **POST method**.
